questions.question: Which body has set up a sub-group for preparing the ‘National Action Plan for Migrant Workers’?
questions.explanation: The NITI Aayog has constituted a sub-group to prepare the ‘National Action Plan for Migrant Workers’, as per the recent announcement of Union Labour Minister Santosh Kumar Gangwar.The sub-group includes members from various ministries, experts, NGOs and other organisations. The Labour Bureau under the Labour Ministry will also begin to conduct an All-India Survey on Migrant workers.

questions.question: Which scheme is implemented at Anganwadi centres to provide health and pre-school education services?
questions.explanation: Under the Integrated Child Development Scheme, a package of six services is provided at various anganwadis, including supplementary nutrition, pre-school non-formal education, immunisation, health education etc.As per the recent data, there has been a steady decline in the number of anganwadi beneficiaries, which dropped to 8.55 crore by March,2020 from 10.45 crore beneficiaries in 2014-15.

questions.question: ‘Formation and Promotion of 10,000’, which was making news recently, belongs to which category of scheme?
questions.explanation: As per the Ministry of Agriculture, a new Central Sector Scheme titled ‘Formation and Promotion of 10,000 Farmer Produce Organizations (FPOs)’ has been launched.The budgetary provision for the scheme is Rs 6,865 crore. The FPOs will be provided financial assistance of up to Rs 18 lakh per organisation for a period of three years.

questions.question: Screenwriter and Academy award winner, Jean-Claude Carriere, who passed away recently, was from which country?
questions.explanation: Veteran novelist and screenwriter from France, Jean-Claude Carriere passed away recently, at the age of 89.He was a prolific writer with a long career spanning six decades. He also won the Oscar award for Best Live Action Short Film and the Oscar Honorary Award (2014) for lifetime achievement. Indian government awarded Padma Shri to him in 2015.

questions.question: What is the name of the Made-in-India alternative to Twitter, which was making news recently?
questions.explanation: Union Ministers and government departments of the country are setting up accounts on Koo application.It is seen as the Made-in-India alternative to the social media giant Twitter. The app was one among the winners of the government’s ‘AatmaNirbhar App Innovation Challenge’ in 2020. Recently, there has been a tussle between Twitter and Indian Government, in matters related to farm protest.

questions.question: ‘The India Toy Fair’ was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: The Government of India has launched the ‘The India Toy Fair 2021’, to promote domestic manufacturing of toys. It is to be held as a digitally accessible exhibition and platform to connect various stakeholders of Toy industry. The programme is scheduled from 27th February to 2nd March 2021. Webinars by State Governments, Knowledge sessions and Competitions are being planned.

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme, launched by Dept. of Science and Technology to encourage girls in STEM field?
questions.explanation: Vigyan Jyoti programme, was launched by the Department of Science & Technology (DST) which encourages gilrs take interest in science and build their career.The second phase of Vigyan Jyoti programme was commenced on the occasion of International Day of Women and Girls in Science on February 11, 2021. This will extend the scheme to 50 more districts in additional to the 50 districts.

questions.question: Applications from Siemens Healthcare and Wipro GE Healthcare have been approved by the Government under which scheme?
questions.explanation: The Government of India has approved applications from leading medical devices manufacturing companies, under the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme, by the Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers.These companies include Siemens Healthcare, Sahajanand Medical Technologies, Nipro India Corporation and Wipro GE Healthcare.

questions.question: As per recent Government data, what percent of India’s prison inmates are illiterate?
questions.explanation: The Union Ministry of Home Affairs have recently released the data pertaining to the literacy levels of prison inmates in India.As per the data, 1,32,729 out of 4,78,600 prison inmates in the country are illiterate. This constitutes 27.37 per cent of the total inmates.

questions.question: In which state the Chittaura Lake is situated?
questions.explanation: Chittaura Jheel or Chittaura Lake is situated in Uttar Pradesh. A river named Teri Nadi originates from this lake. It is known for many migratory birds during August – October months. It is also an important Hindu Pilgrimage site in the state.Prime Minster Narendra Modi is scheduled to inaugurate the development works of the Chittaura Lake.
